fields_json: {"title":"Wildflower honey label","variety":"Wildflower","net_weight":"1 lb","body":"Raw wildflower honey. Fox Chase, Philadelphia. Not heated, not filtered."}
{"title":"Wildflower honey label, half pint","variety":"Wildflower","net_weight":"8 oz","body":"Raw wildflower honey. Fox Chase hives. Crystallizes in cold. Warm jar to clear."}
{"title":"Spring honey label","variety":"Spring blossom","net_weight":"1 lb","body":"Spring harvest. Light, floral. From locust and early clover."}
{"title":"Late summer honey label","variety":"Late summer","net_weight":"1 lb","body":"Late summer harvest. Darker, stronger. Goldenrod and aster."}
{"title":"Comb honey label","variety":"Comb","net_weight":"12 oz","body":"Cut comb in honey. Eat the wax. Limited each year."}
{"title":"Creamed honey label","variety":"Creamed","net_weight":"1 lb","body":"Whipped to a spread. Same raw honey, no additives. Keeps soft."}
{"title":"Beeswax block label","variety":"Beeswax","net_weight":"2 oz","body":"Filtered beeswax. For wraps, polish, candles."}
{"title":"Honey ingredients and storage line","variety":"All varieties","net_weight":"","body":"Ingredients: raw honey. Store at room temp. Not for infants under one year."}
{"title":"Spring 2025 harvest","season":"Spring 2025","body":"Pulled spring supers end of May. Twelve hives, light crop after a cold April. Locust came in strong."}
{"title":"Late summer 2025 harvest","season":"Late summer 2025","body":"Late summer pull mid-August. Good goldenrod flow. Darker than spring, sells well in fall."}
{"title":"Spring 2024 harvest","season":"Spring 2024","body":"Wet spring, slower start. Pulled first week of June. About 250 lbs across the yard."}
{"title":"Late summer 2024 harvest","season":"Late summer 2024","body":"Dry August cut the flow short. Comb honey limited. Still a clean dark crop."}
{"title":"Fall wrap-up 2025","season":"Fall 2025","body":"Last extraction done. Hives fed and wrapped for winter. Roughly 600 lbs for the year."}
{"title":"Winter colony check","season":"Winter 2026","body":"Mid-January check. All twelve clusters alive. Light hefts on two, added fondant."}
{"title":"Spring buildup 2026","season":"Spring 2026","body":"Hives building fast on maple and willow. Supers on by April if weather holds."}
{"title":"Where to find the honey","heading":"Where to buy","body":"Briarwood Farmers Market, Saturdays April through October, 7 to 1. Also Cedarbrook General Store and Ridgemont Co-op."}
{"title":"About the hives","heading":"About","body":"Twelve colonies in Fox Chase, Philadelphia. Small operation. Honey raw, local, sold by the season."}
{"title":"Why it crystallizes","heading":"On crystallization","body":"Raw honey crystallizes. That's normal. Set the jar in warm water to bring it back."}
{"title":"Wholesale inquiries","heading":"For shops","body":"Cases by the season. Email for current varieties and pricing."}
{"title":"Holiday jars","heading":"Holiday orders","body":"Order holiday jars by mid-December. Gift labels on request."}
